intended_use: Tempus ECG-AF is intended for use to analyze recordings of 12-lead ECG devices and detect signs associated with a patient experiencing atrial fibrillation and/or atrial flutter (collectively referred
intended_use_full: Tempus ECG-AF is intended for use to analyze recordings of 12-lead ECG devices and detect signs associated with a patient experiencing atrial fibrillation and/or atrial flutter (collectively referred to as AF) within the next 12 months. It is for use on resting 12-lead ECG recordings collected at a healthcare facility from patients: 65 years of age or older, without pre-existing or concurrent documentation of atrial fibrillation and/or atrial flutter, who do not have a pacemaker or implantable cardioverter defibrillator, and who did not have cardiac surgery within the preceding 30 days.
target_condition: Atrial fibrillation and/or atrial flutter (AF) occurring within 12 months of ECG analysis
ground_truth: Clinical diagnosis of AF determined by duplicate manual chart review over at least 1 year of follow-up
other_metrics: 95% CI for sensitivity: 25%-37%; 95% CI for specificity: 91%-92%; 95% CI for PPV: 15%-23%; 95% CI for NPV: 95%-96%; 1-year incidence of clinical diagnosis of AF in study population: 6.0%
